key_points: Life processes are the basic functions performed by living organisms to survive on Earth.
Nutrition involves taking in food and its utilization by the body for energy and growth.
Respiration is the process of breaking down food to release energy using oxygen.
Circulation is the transport of nutrients, oxygen, and waste products within the body.
Excretion is the removal of harmful metabolic waste products from the body.
examples: Digestion: Breaking down complex starch into simple sugars like glucose.
Breathing Organs: Humans use lungs, fish use gills, and insects use spiracles.
Transport: Blood carrying oxygen from the lungs to all parts of the body.
Excretion: Sweating and urinating to remove excess salts and urea.
formulas: Cellular Respiration: Glucose + Oxygen → Carbon Dioxide + Water + Energy
Human Heart Rate: Average 72 to 80 beats per minute
exam_tips: Remember that digestion starts in the mouth, not the stomach.
Identify the heart as the muscular pump of the circulatory system.
Note that the kidneys are the primary organs for filtering waste from the blood.
